sentences.text: My family still live in Charleston.
But yeah, they I'd always go up to visit them.
No, I've just moved there actually, recently.
What problem there, if you don't mind me asking.
I'm not too far from you.
Are you located in Charleston as well?
Or where are you about getting so much snow?
This is probably gonna be a super quick and straightforward call.
Basically wanted to get us all together to just determine a couple of things on our end.
Just kind of what your goals are with the product.
And then Daniel has just some more technical questions that she needs to ask but first I would love to see here just a very quick, high, level overview of Mcgrath properties.
What you guys have in your portfolio, and we can just start there if you don't mind.
So we're smaller company based out of Madison, Wisconsin.
We've got 9 properties right now, with the tenth one opening this June brings us just over a thousand apartments.
So we're primarily multi family housing with some commercial space as well.
And I am pulling up the contract now, Danielle.
But we're not bringing on all 9 for reputation.
What is the reasoning behind bringing on those 6?
So we were kinda trying to.
This is something new we're trying.
So we wanted to see how it goes.
Try it out on some of our buildings that we think could use it more.
And then, once that we could cross market on better.
Okay, perfect and the buildings that you're putting their view management on.
Can you tell me a little bit about their history?
Let me pull it up quick.
The one opening is on there.
So that one opens June first.
That one is, though probably the only one you guys don't have access to for this just because it is so new.
We don't have a name yet, and we haven't started the Google account yet.
That one we're still working on.
And then the other ones kind of range.
We've got our 6 40 West properties, the oldest that's gonna be on there, and that one open into 2,013 and then they all kind of like.
There's the 20 threer that one open 2013 factor district 2016 the old warehouse, 1 51 East Wilson, both open 2018 and urban.
I was curious because everybody has different goals when it comes to reputation management?
What are you guys primarily focused on?
Is it really kind of just solicitating and getting reviews in?
Or is it more so increasing your star ratings, or a little bit of both
I mean, we really like don't get many reviews at all, and the ones we do get are the typically disgruntled tenants that are kind of matted us, no matter what.
So ideally, we could get some of these to have better reviews, and also just more reviews in general.
Danielle, what am I missing here?
Anything else in regards to that?
That we that we need to know.
I think you kind of hit the hit all all aspects there.
And I just have a couple of questions.
They will be pretty quick and easy.
Do you use any like Crm or like syndication to keep track of, like your tenants like real page.
any sort of that like a property management software.
I ask, because we have 2 options here.
There's so in order to like send campaigns.
To those review requests, we have the option to do more of a manual route where you'll be able to upload contacts directly into your dashboard.
And then once those contacts are uploaded that email requesting for a review will automatically go out otherwise for app folio.
We don't have a direct syndication with them, but we can set up a well, you can set up a report to be sent to our support team like once a week.
We have clients typically do like Monday mornings.
And then that way our our support team can go ahead, upload the contacts directly and then have that campaign.
Send out Do you have a preference there?
It's kind of up to you if you wanted a little more hands off or to be more involved.
I don't need an answer right now, either we might have to think about it.
Follow up email, and I'll list the 2 options so you can think about it.
So yeah, yeah, definitely, I mean, one thing that we can always do is change it.
So we can start out with the manual process.
And then, if you're like, this is kind of taken more time than what I would like, then we can switch it to that report style.
Otherwise we can obviously do the opposite.
And then my next question is, will it be you 2 that are the primary users that need access to the account, or will there be anyone else?
There'll be more people as well.
We have 2 senior property managers that we'd want on there.
then, what I'm going to do with my follow up email, is, send you a spreadsheet if you can just include their first name, last name emails.
And then, if they should a have access to like a specific property I can set that up.
And then I think next week, if you have any availability, then we can go ahead and do our initial training and then at that time I like to just like Poke through the account.
Have you set up with access so you can kinda go through with me, and then the next meeting will go ahead and send that initial campaign requesting reviews.
Question about like timing of sort of sending out review requests.
Like the highest conversion rate, like where people actually click on the you know, link and leave review.
Is it like when people are moving in when they're moving out?

Do you just like, do people have the most success when they just like blast people not stop with reviewing until they fill it out like, what's what's the most successful way to get people to leave reviews.
So we typically see people do like after a move in after a work order is closed and then after move out.
Sometimes people do like after tour as well.
But it's completely up to you.
I would not recommend just like blasting them with review requests until they leave a review.
We typically set it up that they the tenants can't be contacted twice within 30 days.
I know personally, if I'm getting a bunch of emails requesting a review on like, I don't wanna do that, you know.
So we like to space it out.
Typically, yeah, I'd recommend, if you're like resending to the same people.
I would do like once a quarter and then our system, though, will not send.
If someone does leave a review under an email, it will not like send a review request again, which is nice, so it can like flag.
That does that kind of answer your question.
You you mentioned like some some clients send you like a report every week, and where you'd send out reviews.
So I did like, if you, if we send out like a report of our you know, tenants every week, are you gonna be sending out like an email blast which feels very overwhelming for people.
yeah, so typically our other clients that are on it folio, they their report has.
It's like a unique list every week.
So it's not the same tenants my team does like cross reference, and they make sure that the same name isn't added twice within the span of a couple of weeks.
But our system, like, I said, also, like blocks, a communication from being like sent out multiple times within 30 days.
So it's a list of like move ins for that week or move out.
Yeah, yeah, it's like, completely customizable.
Carly and I had a meeting earlier today.

And those clients were like, we don't really want to send review requests to people who just moved out because they're that in their situation they weren't as pleased so that we want to send them for people that are like successful work.
Orders are closed or like, right when they move in, since it's like starting off with a fresh foot.
Then do we want to look at our calendars and kinda try to figure out a time for next week that works for everyone to do that initial training?
Is there a day in particular next week that works best for you guys
I would say, my Thursday is really open.
So that would work really well for me if that works.
Yeah, that works for me as well.
I have an appointment in the afternoon in Madison.
But I could do between one and 2 on Thursday meetings all morning
I think one of our one of our Cpms.
Has a an appointment at one.
I guess she could possibly switch that to someone else.
I'm available like one until 2, 30 or 9 until noon.
So definitely open more open on Tuesday
Well, one of us will send an invite for that time, but if anything changes between now and then, of course, just let us know we can move it so
Have a good weekend, and we'll talk to you next week, if not sooner.
